titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Patricia McGowan Wald Papers
titlestmt.author: complied by staff of Manuscripts and Archives

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday April 11, 2024 at 07:00

did.unittitle: Patricia McGowan Wald papers

descriptivenote.p: The materials are predominantly in English.

did.abstract: The papers consist of judicial and personal papers of Patricia M. Wald, including chief judge materials, general chambers files, closed case files, correspondence, subject files, speeches, and publications. Also included are trial transcripts, trial exhibits, and memoranda from the International Criminal Tribunal for the Former Yugoslavia, and other materials documenting Judge Wald's legal work after retiring from active service on the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Permission for access must be obtained from the donor's daughter, Sarah E. Wald, until January 12, 2029. In addition, all files relating to Wald's tenure on the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit are closed until the judges who served with Wald have retired.

The material is unprocessed and may contain sensitive information or be in a physical state that would prohibit use. Researchers wishing to request access should email beinecke.library@yale.edu to initiate the review process, which may take several weeks.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright is retained by the creator(s) of this collection for materials they have authored or otherwise produced. After the lifetime of the creator(s), copyright passes to Yale University. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Patricia McGowan Wald Papers (MS 1631). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Patricia McGowan Wald was born in Torrington, Connecticut in 1928. She received a BA degree from Connecticut College in 1948 and an LLB from the Yale Law School in 1951. From 1951 to 1952, Wald served as a law clerk to Judge Jerome Frank and from 1952 to 1953 was an associate in the firm Arnold, Fortas and Porter. During the 1960s, Wald held several conference and commission positions studying crime, violence, civil disorder, criminal justice, and law and poverty. She also served as an attorney with the Neighborhood Legal Services Program and the Center for Law and Social Policy in Washington, D.C. and was co-chair of the Ford Foundation Drug Abuse Research Project. From 1972 to 1977, Wald was an attorney for the Mental Health Law project, and she served as the Project's litigation director from 1975 to 1977. Wald was the assistant attorney general for legislative affairs in the Department of Justice from 1977 to 1979. She was appointed circuit judge for the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit in 1979 and served as its chief judge from 1986 to 1991. Wald served on the United Nations' International Criminal Tribunal for the Former Yugoslavia from 1999 to 2002, on the President's Commission on Intelligence Capabilities of the United States Regarding Weapons of Mass Destruction from 2004 to 2005, and the Privacy and Civil Liberties Oversight Board from 2012 to 2017. Wald died in Washington, D.C. on January 12, 2019.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of judicial and personal papers of Patricia M. Wald, including chief judge materials, general chambers files, closed case files, correspondence, subject files, speeches, and publications. Also included are trial transcripts, trial exhibits, and memoranda from the International Criminal Tribunal for the Former Yugoslavia, and other materials documenting Judge Wald's legal work after retiring from active service on the United States Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia Circuit.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The material is arranged as received from the donor.

corpname.part: United States. Circuit Court (District of Columbia)
International Tribunal for the Prosecution of Persons Responsible for Serious Violations of International Humanitarian Law Committed in the Territory of the Former Yugoslavia since 1991
